name: Greens Indoor Golf & Cafe
about: Greens Indoor Golf & Cafe is an unstaffed facility offering two aboutGolf™ simulator studios with over 40 PGA tour courses and practice ranges. It features competitive pricing with no membership or initiation fees, starting at $50 per hour, with discounts available for multi-hour bookings. The venue operates on a self-guided model, allowing players to use the equipment independently with remote assistance available if needed. It is an ideal destination for individuals, groups, or families looking for a flexible and affordable golf simulation experience.
why_golfers_like_it: Unstaffed facility with flexible booking and 10 minutes of free warm-up time
Competitive rates starting at $35/hour for packages, with no membership fees
BYOB policy with refrigerators in each simulator studio for food and drinks
Lefty and righty golf clubs available at no extra charge
Advanced 3Trak technology for accurate shot tracking

food_and_drink.notes: BYO food allowed; Green Point Juicery serves food and juices before 5 PM
BYOB facility permitted for those 21 and older; no alcohol sold on-site

simulator_hardware.notes: Features advanced 3Trak technology for shot tracking

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: No, complimentary use of golf clubs (lefties, righties, men, women, and kids sizes) is provided at no charge. You are also welcome to bring your own clubs.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility is BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverages) and allows you to bring your own food. Each simulator studio has a refrigerator for your beverages.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: It takes approximately 1 hour for an individual player to play 18 holes. For groups, it is recommended to book an additional hour per player (e.g., a foursome should book 4 hours).

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Minors can play but only when supervised by an adult, as golf can be a dangerous activity for young players. Parents are responsible for their children's safety.
